sources.title: JSXGraph 1.12.2 API Reference
sources.summary: Full API reference for JSXGraph 1.12.2. Confirms element types: CurveIntersection, Derivative, Tracecurve, Intersection, Functiongraph, Glider. JXG.Math.Numerics contains generalizedNewton and chandrupatla root-finding algorithms used internally.

claims.text: JSXGraph 1.12.2 has a Derivative element that computes the numerical derivative of any JXG.Curve, including functiongraph. Usage: board.create('derivative', [curve], {dash: 2})

JSXGraph CurveIntersection creates the REGION intersection of two closed paths (Curve/Polygon/Circle), not individual intersection points. Usage: board.create('curveintersection', [curve1, curve2], {fillColor: 'yellow', fillOpacity: 0.6})

JSXGraph Tracecurve traces the locus of a point as a Glider sweeps its constraint. Usage: board.create('tracecurve', [glider, tracedPoint]). numberPoints field controls curve resolution. LIMITATION: mover must be a Glider, not a freely dragged functiongraph.

JXG.Math.Numerics provides: generalizedNewton(c1, c2, t1ini, t2ini), generalizedDampedNewtonCurves(c1, c2, t1ini, t2ini, gamma, eps), root(f, x) as chandrupatla alias, Newton(f, x) for single-variable root-finding. JSXGraph uses these internally for the intersection element.
board.reducedUpdate property: 'If reducedUpdate is set to true then only the dragged element and few (dependent) elements are updated.' Critical for 60fps with 10+ elements.
board.minimizeReflow attribute changes the redraw strategy in the SVG rendering engine. Available as board-level configuration for performance tuning.
board.isSuspendedRedraw: flag set to true when suspendUpdate() is active, skipping all redraws until unsuspendUpdate() is called.
Board modes: BOARD_MODE_DRAG (during drag, objects not highlighted), BOARD_MODE_NONE (normal), BOARD_MODE_MOVE_ORIGIN (panning). board.mouse references the currently dragged object.

sources.title: JSXGraph GitHub Releases (v1.9.0-v1.12.2)
sources.summary: Release notes confirm: v1.12.1 increased precision for intersections of curves with curves or lines (BREAKING CHANGE WARNING). v1.11.0 added CSS class binding to elements enabling SVG filters. v1.9.0 overhauled otherintersection to support forbidden intersection arrays.

claims.text: JSXGraph v1.12.1 release: 'Increased precision for intersections (points) of curves with curves or lines.' The intersection algorithm for curves was changed — may affect existing constructions. This confirms the Intersection element supports functiongraph inputs with individual POINTS.

JSXGraph v1.11.0: 'ability to pass the hosting element as a parameter to functions given as values of attributes' — enables context-aware attribute callbacks on any element. Also: CSS classes can now be bound to all JSXGraph elements, enabling SVG filter integration.

JSXGraph v1.9.0: otherintersection element overhauled — 'Now it is possible to supply an array of points as forbidden intersection points. This makes it very convenient to visualize situations where more than two intersections arise.' Relevant for multiple functiongraph intersections.
JSXGraph v1.12.2 is identical to v1.12.1, only removing zip files for CDN (jsdelivr) size limits. Functional API is v1.12.1.

sources.title: JSXGraph board.on('update') Event Pattern
sources.summary: Confirmed board.on('update', callback) fires on every board update including drag operations. Can update external HTML elements with coordinate values. Also confirmed element.on('drag/move/up/down') event system from GeometryElement.

claims.text: board.on('update', function(){ /* read element coords, update labels */ }) fires on every board update. Inside callback, p.X() and p.Y() return current coordinates. Example: board.on('update', function(){ document.getElementById('D').value = g4.X(); })
GeometryElement events: 'attribute', 'attribute:key', 'down', 'drag', 'move', 'up', 'over', 'out'. All elements including functiongraph inherit these via JXG.EventEmitter.
JXG.addEvent(element.rendNode, 'mouseover', callback, element) works with SVG/VML renderer only. JSXGraph's native .on() system works with Canvas renderer too.

sources.title: JSXGraph Dynamic Text and Draggable Function Patterns
sources.summary: Dynamic text bound to function coordinates: board.create('text', [xFn, y, contentFn]). Draggable functiongraph defined as function of draggable point's coordinates: auto-updates all dependents via JSXGraph's native dependency system.

claims.text: Dynamic text pattern: board.create('text', [function(){ return p.X() - 0.17; }, 2, function(){ return '$' + Math.floor(p.X() * 10) / 10 + '$'; }]) creates text that auto-updates when point p is dragged.

Draggable functiongraph via parent point: define functiongraph as function using A.X() and A.Y(), then create text reading same values. When A is dragged, JSXGraph's native dependency chain auto-updates graph + text + all dependents without manual board.update() calls.
Opacity as a function pattern: board.create('text', [x, y, msg], { opacity: function(){ return condition ? 1 : 0; }}) enables conditional element visibility triggered by drag state.

sources.title: JSXGraph suspendUpdate/unsuspendUpdate + Locus Pattern
sources.summary: board.suspendUpdate() / board.unsuspendUpdate() batch update pattern confirmed in watts_curve tracecurve example. Locus element: board.createElement('locus', [point]) creates geometric locus. Intersection of geometric elements: board.createElement('intersection', [c2,c3,0]) selects index 0 of two intersection points.

claims.text: batch update pattern: board.suspendUpdate() before creating/mutating multiple elements, board.unsuspendUpdate() after. Prevents N intermediate redraws during construction. Example from watts_curve: suspendUpdate() before 7 element creations, unsuspendUpdate() after.

Native intersection of two circles with index selection: board.createElement('intersection', [c2, c3, 0]) returns the first intersection point, board.createElement('intersection', [c2, c3, 1]) returns the second. Index is the third argument.

Locus element: board.createElement('locus', [midpoint], {strokeColor: 'red'}) traces the geometric locus of a point as a glider moves. Different from Tracecurve: locus is computed analytically, tracecurve is sampled numerically.

sources.title: JSXGraph Wiki: Intersection of functiongraphs
sources.summary: Official wiki example shows CurveIntersection (region) for functiongraphs, NOT individual points. To get actual intersection POINTS with coordinates, must use JXG.Math.Numerics.root() with f(x)-g(x)=0, or rely on board.create('intersection', ...) which v1.12.1 improved for curve-curve.

claims.text: CurveIntersection of functiongraphs creates filled intersection REGION, not POINTS. Example: board.create('curveintersection', [curve1, curve2], {fillColor:'yellow', fillOpacity:0.3}). This is useful for teaching |f(x)| fold regions or showing overlap between curves visually.

There is NO official JSXGraph wiki example for intersection POINTS of two functiongraphs. The standard approach is: (1) JXG.Math.Numerics.root(function(x){ return f(x)-g(x); }, initialGuess) to find x, then create a dependent JXG point at (x, f(x)). Or use board.create('intersection', [fg1, fg2, 0]) which v1.12.1 improved.

sources.title: JSXGraph Glider Element with snap and animation
sources.summary: Glider is a movable point constrained to a curve/line. Supports: snap-to-grid (snapsizex/snapsizey), snap-to-points (snaptopoints), animation (animateanimated, animatespeed, animatestart/stop/loop/direction), fixed mode. Can serve as drag handle for functiongraph translation.

claims.text: Glider elements support snap-to-grid: snapsizex/snapsizey parameters. snaptopoints: true snaps to existing points. These behaviors make gliders suitable as constrained drag handles for teaching demonstrations.
Glider infobox: showinfobox: true displays coordinate info. infoboxdigits: 'auto' controls decimal precision. This enables automatic coordinate readout during drag without custom text elements.

sources.title: JSXGraph JXG.Math.Numerics API Reference
sources.summary: Full numerics API. Root-finding: chandrupatla (via root()), Newton(), fzero(). Curve intersection: generalizedNewton(c1,c2,t1ini,t2ini), generalizedDampedNewtonCurves(c1,c2,t1ini,t2ini,gamma,eps). Integration: GaussLegendre, Romberg, NewtonCotes, Qag. Path simplification: RamerDouglasPeucker.

claims.text: JXG.Math.Numerics.generalizedNewton(c1, c2, t1ini, t2ini): Compute intersection of curves c1 and c2 using generalized Newton-Raphson. t1ini, t2ini are initial parameter guesses. Returns intersection point.

JXG.Math.Numerics.root(f, x, context): abstract method for univariate root finding, alias for chandrupatla. Chandrupatla combines bisection, secant, and inverse quadratic interpolation — more robust than Newton for functions without derivatives. maxIterationsRoot configures max iterations.

For real-time functiongraph intersection during drag: use JXG.Math.Numerics.root(function(x){ return f(x) - g(x); }, lastKnownX) where lastKnownX is the previous intersection x-coordinate as warm start. This enables efficient re-rooting near the previous position.
claims.note: [Inference] — derived from API capability, not from an official usage example for this exact pattern

sources.title: DevCon 5 Ontology Design Principles and 3-Phase Journey
sources.summary: DevCon 5 (March 2026): Four design principles (DDD/DRY/OCP/PECS). 3-phase product journey: Golden Tables → Operational Decision-Making → AI-First. palantir-math Sequencer is at Phase 2. Key primitives: Interfaces, Structs, Reducers, Derived Properties, Object-Backed Link Types.

claims.text: [§DC5-05] Four ontology design principles: (1) Domain-Driven Design, (2) Don't Repeat Yourself (rule of three), (3) Open for extension, closed for modification, (4) Producer Extends, Consumer Super (PECS). These ARE software design principles applied to ontology.

[§DC5-04] palantir-math Sequencer JSXGraph enhancement is at Phase 2 — Operational Decision-Making: data shapes (JSXGraphScene) exist, now encoding kinetics (real-time intersection logic, drag cascade, dependent element chain) and interactive actions (drag → update → redraw).

[§DC5-03] Real-time decision space encoding: 'fragmented enterprise data must be represented at maximum fidelity.' Applied to canvas: drag position + intersection coordinates + annotation state must be captured at every drag frame for teaching-grade fidelity.

[§DC5-06] Key ontology primitives for canvas enhancement: Reducers (collapse multi-value drag history into canonical drag state), Derived Properties (intersection coordinates derived from functiongraph positions), Interfaces (polymorphic drag behavior across Linear/Quadratic/Abs graph families).

sources.title: Palantir Ontology Model — SENSE→DECIDE→ACT→LEARN Digital Twin
sources.summary: ARCH-05: Digital Twin = SENSE → DECIDE → ACT → LEARN loop. ARCH-14/15/16: DATA/LOGIC/ACTION domain classification. ARCH-21: Schema = Contract + Instruction + Verification.

claims.text: [§ARCH-05] Digital Twin: SENSE = capture drag event (element position, delta), DECIDE = compute intersection (Newton-Raphson), ACT = update canvas (label coords, ghost curve, derivative), LEARN = record interaction telemetry (drag path, intersection events). All four phases must be covered for complete JSXGraph enhancement.

[§ARCH-15] LOGIC domain = reasoning layer. IntersectionSolver, AbsValueFoldComputer, DependencyGraph, DragSemanticResolver all belong to LOGIC. They derive facts from DATA but do not persist state directly.
[§ARCH-16] ACTION domain = mutations. DragCascadeUpdate, BatchBoardUpdate, AnnotationToggle, TraceCurveRecord are ACTION-domain operations — they change the canvas reality when executed.

sources.title: Absolute Value Fold Visualization via Ghost Curve Pattern
sources.summary: Synthesized from JSXGraph functiongraph + CurveDifference/CurveIntersection API. |f(x)| fold visualization: create ghost curve as y = -f(x) for x where f(x)<0. Fold line at y=0. Highlight with CurveDifference for region below axis. Dynamic update via translationVector on functiongraph.

claims.text: [Synthesis] Absolute value visualization approach: (A) Primary curve f(x), (B) Ghost curve |f(x)| = Math.abs(f(x)), (C) Reflection guide: curve y = -f(x) for negative region (shown dashed), (D) Fold boundary: line at y=0 highlighted between x-intercepts. When f(x) translates vertically (ty parameter), all four update via shared ty closure.

[Synthesis] Real-time absolute value fold during drag: define absGraph = board.create('functiongraph', [function(x){ return Math.abs(f_raw(x) + ty); }]). When ty changes via drag, both f(x)+ty and |f(x)+ty| update simultaneously if both read the same ty variable.

sources.title: JSXGraph SVG Performance: minimizeReflow + reducedUpdate
sources.summary: Board-level performance controls: reducedUpdate (only update dragged element + direct deps), minimizeReflow (SVG reflow reduction), isSuspendedRedraw (skip all redraws). Critical for 60fps with 10+ interactive elements.

claims.text: JSXGraph SVG renderer performance controls: (1) board.reducedUpdate — only recompute dragged element chain, (2) board.suspendUpdate() / board.unsuspendUpdate() — batch mutations, (3) board.minimizeReflow — SVG-specific reflow reduction. All three must be considered for 10+ element 60fps target.

[Inference] For real-time drag with 10+ elements: the critical bottleneck is the intersection recomputation on each mouse-move event. Newton-Raphson with warm start (previous intersection x as initial guess) should complete in <1ms for smooth polynomials, well within 16ms frame budget.

sources.title: JSXGraph Trace Curve and Locus Patterns
sources.summary: Tracecurve requires Glider as mover. Not directly applicable to functiongraph drag. Alternative for drag-path recording: use board.on('update') to push current position to a data array, then update a curve.dataX/dataY manually.

claims.text: Tracecurve: board.create('tracecurve', [C_glider, S_dependent]) where C_glider is a Glider element and S_dependent is a point whose locus is traced. LIMITATION: C must be a Glider on a constraint. Cannot use for free drag of functiongraphs.

[Synthesis] For functiongraph drag-path recording: create a curve with empty dataX/dataY, then in board.on('update') or element.on('drag'), push current graph position to dataX/dataY arrays and call curve.update(). This gives a hand-drawn trace effect without JSXGraph-native tracecurve.

sources.title: Palantir Builder Surface Principles from Orchestration Map
sources.summary: ORCH-03/ORCH-10: Builder surfaces must be explicit, not implicit. ORCH-10 rule: 'Builder surfaces are explicit, not implicit.' Applies to JSXGraph canvas: teaching annotation overlay, coordinate labels, and interaction affordances must be declared elements, not side effects.

claims.text: [§ORCH-10] Rule: 'Builder surfaces are explicit, not implicit.' Applied to JSXGraph teaching canvas: every annotation element (coordinate label, fold line, derivative display, trace overlay) must be a declared board element with explicit lifecycle, not an ad-hoc DOM mutation.

evidence.quote: Increased precision for intersections (points) of curves with curves or lines
evidence.note: v1.12.1 improved native Intersection element for curve-curve — palantir-math already at 1.12.2

evidence.quote: generalizedNewton(c1, c2, t1ini, t2ini): Compute an intersection of the curves c1 and c2 with a generalized Newton method
evidence.note: Available as direct call for custom intersection solver
board.on('update') fires during drag — can trigger intersection recompute

evidence.quote: board.suspendUpdate() before N element creations, board.unsuspendUpdate() after
evidence.note: BatchBoardUpdate pattern for dependent cascade mutation

evidence.quote: reducedUpdate: If reducedUpdate is set to true then only the dragged element and few dependent elements are updated
evidence.note: Critical performance flag for drag scenarios

evidence.quote: [Synthesis] Ghost curve |f(x)| via Math.abs wrapper + reflection guide at y=0
board.create('text', [function(){ return p.X()-0.17; }, 2, function(){ return '$' + pos + '$'; }])
evidence.note: Dynamic coordinate labels for teaching annotation
Native derivative element — unused in current palantir-math

evidence.quote: Tracecurve: board.create('tracecurve', [C_glider, S_dependent]). LIMITATION: mover must be a Glider.
evidence.note: Tracecurve cannot be used directly for functiongraph drag history — needs custom array approach

q-jg-01.summary: board.on('update', cb) fires every drag frame. element.on('drag', cb) at element level. board.suspendUpdate()/unsuspendUpdate() for batch. board.reducedUpdate=true for performance. Native dependency chain: functiongraph defined as function(x){...using A.X()/A.Y()...} auto-updates all dependents when A is dragged.

q-jg-02.summary: Two approaches: (A) JSXGraph native Intersection element: board.create('intersection', [fg1, fg2, 0]) — v1.12.1 improved precision for curve-curve. Auto-updates as functions change IF functiongraphs are defined with JSXGraph-native dependency (via parent point). (B) Custom solver: JXG.Math.Numerics.root(function(x){return f(x)-g(x);}, warmStart) called in board.on('update'). Warm start (previous intersection x) is critical for <1ms performance.

q-jg-03.summary: Three visual layers: (1) primary f(x)+ty curve, (2) |f(x)+ty| abs curve via Math.abs wrapper sharing ty variable, (3) reflection guide: dashed y=-f(x) for negative fold region, (4) fold boundary: line at y=0 between x-intercepts. All four share ty closure — update together on drag.

q-jg-04.summary: Unused native elements: Derivative (numerical derivative curve), OtherIntersection (forbidden-point intersection), CurveDifference/CurveUnion (set operations for curve regions), Transform elements, Integral (area under curve). Tracecurve requires Glider mover — not directly applicable.

q-jg-05.summary: Dynamic text: board.create('text', [xFn, y, contentFn]) — auto-updates on every board.update(). Coordinate labels bound to intersection point: board.create('text', [function(){return intersectPt.X();}, function(){return intersectPt.Y()+0.3;}, function(){return '('+intersectPt.X().toFixed(2)+', '+intersectPt.Y().toFixed(2)+')';}]). Glider infobox (showinfobox:true) for built-in coordinate display.

q-jg-06.summary: Performance stack: (1) board.reducedUpdate=true during drag, (2) batch mutations with suspendUpdate/unsuspendUpdate, (3) board.minimizeReflow for SVG, (4) Newton warm start for intersection (<1ms for polynomials), (5) throttle board.on('update') to requestAnimationFrame if needed.

q-jg-07.summary: graphFamily.ts extension: DDD principle suggests new Interface for DragBehavior per graph family (Linear/Quadratic/Cubic/Abs/Piecewise/Parametric). OCP: open for new families without modifying existing drag semantics. Parametric drag needs separate x(t)/y(t) drag model. Piecewise needs segment-aware drag constraint.
